title: Social Media Influencers
description: You are given four tables: users, posts, comments, and follows. Find names of users who have more than 2 followers AND have at least one post with more than 5 likes, ordered alphabetically.
inputFormat:
users(id, name, joined_year)
posts(id, user_id, content, likes)
comments(id, post_id, commenter_id, body)
follows(id, follower_id, followed_id)
outputFormat: One user name per line, ordered alphabetically.
constraints: Use JOINs and GROUP BY. Use HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT follower_id) > 2. Filter WHERE likes > 5. Include ORDER BY name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT DISTINCT u.name FROM users u JOIN follows f ON u.id = f.followed_id JOIN posts p ON u.id = p.user_id WHERE p.likes > 5 GROUP BY u.id, u.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT f.follower_id) > 2 ORDER BY u.name;
hint: Join users with follows (as followed) and with posts, filter posts with likes > 5, GROUP BY user, HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT follower_id) > 2.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER, name TEXT, joined_year INTEGER); CREATE TABLE posts (id INTEGER, user_id INTEGER, content TEXT, likes INTEGER); CREATE TABLE comments (id INTEGER, post_id INTEGER, commenter_id INTEGER, body TEXT); CREATE TABLE follows (id INTEGER, follower_id INTEGER, followed_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO users VALUES (1,'Alice',2020),(2,'Bob',2021),(3,'Carol',2022),(4,'Dan',2019); INSERT INTO posts VALUES (1,1,'Post A',8),(2,1,'Post B',3),(3,2,'Post C',7),(4,3,'Post D',2); INSERT INTO comments VALUES (1,1,2,'Nice'),(2,1,3,'Great'); INSERT INTO follows VALUES (1,2,1),(2,3,1),(3,4,1),(4,1,2),(5,4,2);

CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER, name TEXT, joined_year INTEGER); CREATE TABLE posts (id INTEGER, user_id INTEGER, content TEXT, likes INTEGER); CREATE TABLE comments (id INTEGER, post_id INTEGER, commenter_id INTEGER, body TEXT); CREATE TABLE follows (id INTEGER, follower_id INTEGER, followed_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO users VALUES (1,'Zara',2020),(2,'Kai',2021),(3,'Leo',2022),(4,'Mia',2019),(5,'Nora',2023); INSERT INTO posts VALUES (1,1,'Z post',10),(2,2,'K post',6),(3,3,'L post',4),(4,5,'N post',9); INSERT INTO comments VALUES (1,1,2,'Wow'),(2,4,1,'Amazing'); INSERT INTO follows VALUES (1,2,1),(2,3,1),(3,4,1),(4,5,2),(5,4,5),(6,3,5),(7,1,5);

CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER, name TEXT, joined_year INTEGER); CREATE TABLE posts (id INTEGER, user_id INTEGER, content TEXT, likes INTEGER); CREATE TABLE comments (id INTEGER, post_id INTEGER, commenter_id INTEGER, body TEXT); CREATE TABLE follows (id INTEGER, follower_id INTEGER, followed_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO users VALUES (1,'Hank',2022),(2,'Iris',2023); INSERT INTO posts VALUES (1,1,'H post',10),(2,2,'I post',8); INSERT INTO follows VALUES (1,2,1),(2,1,2);

CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER, name TEXT, joined_year INTEGER); CREATE TABLE posts (id INTEGER, user_id INTEGER, content TEXT, likes INTEGER); CREATE TABLE comments (id INTEGER, post_id INTEGER, commenter_id INTEGER, body TEXT); CREATE TABLE follows (id INTEGER, follower_id INTEGER, followed_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO users VALUES (1,'Jack',2019),(2,'Kate',2020),(3,'Liam',2021),(4,'Maya',2022),(5,'Nick',2023); INSERT INTO posts VALUES (1,1,'J post',10),(2,2,'K post',7),(3,3,'L post',6); INSERT INTO follows VALUES (1,2,1),(2,3,1),(3,4,1),(4,1,2),(5,3,2),(6,4,2),(7,1,3),(8,2,3),(9,4,3);

CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER, name TEXT, joined_year INTEGER); CREATE TABLE posts (id INTEGER, user_id INTEGER, content TEXT, likes INTEGER); CREATE TABLE comments (id INTEGER, post_id INTEGER, commenter_id INTEGER, body TEXT); CREATE TABLE follows (id INTEGER, follower_id INTEGER, followed_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO users VALUES (1,'Owen',2021),(2,'Pam',2022),(3,'Quinn',2023); INSERT INTO posts VALUES (1,1,'O post',2),(2,2,'P post',15),(3,3,'Q post',3); INSERT INTO follows VALUES (1,2,2),(2,3,2),(3,1,2);
